label: ∅ | live | warrior
sentence: Long ago, in a village beside a great river, there lived a young warrior who had fallen into hardship.

label: spirit | be | ∅
sentence: His spirit was strong, but his hands were empty.

label: hunger | grow | ∅
sentence: The hunger in his heart grew, and he asked his father’s blessing to leave the lodge and travel, hoping to find a living by the power of his own arm.

label: father | say | son
sentence: The father, wise with years like the ancient trees, said: “Son, place the feet of contentment beneath the blanket of safety.

label: elder | speak | ∅
sentence: The elders have spoken that joy is not born from endless struggle, and the greatest medicine for want is to hold one’s desires lightly.

label: one | seize | fortune
sentence: No one seizes fortune like snaring the wind.

label: arrow | pierce | sky
sentence: No arrows pierce the sky with empty hands.

label: you | carry | skill
sentence:
If you carry a hundred skills, but the spirits do not favor you,
Your strength will be no shield against hardship.

label: warrior | do | what
sentence: What can a warrior do against a cruel wind?

label: hand | be | ∅
sentence: The hand of fate is stronger than the hand of a bear.”

label: son | answer | ∅
sentence: The son answered: “Father, traveling brings many gifts—renewing the spirit, learning the songs of other tribes, meeting friends, gaining honor, and seeing the world’s great wonders.

label: you | become | eagle
sentence:
The journey shapes a man, as the traveling shamans teach:

If you never leave your fire circle,
You will never become the eagle who soars.

label: ∅ | walk | land
sentence:
Walk the land far and wide,
Before you walk the final path.”

label: father | nod | ∅
sentence: The father nodded and replied, “The gifts of travel bless only a few: first, the wealthy trader whose tents rise in every village, surrounded by helpers and guarded by strong warriors.

label: he | honor | ∅
sentence: Wherever he travels, he is honored.

label: man | be | stranger
sentence: But the poor man is a stranger even in his own village.

label: people | honor | presence
sentence: The people honor such a presence like shining obsidian.

label: man | be | sun
sentence:
A learned man is like the sun on the morning dew,
Seen and treasured wherever he treads.

label: fool | be | crow
sentence:
But a fool, even if born a chief’s son,
Is like a black crow lost among eagles.

label: face | be | gift
sentence:
A warrior's face is a gift to all who meet him,
A precious shell sought by many.

label: song | be | medicine
sentence:
Sweet songs are medicine to the soul,
Bringing joy that no blade can cut.

label: ∅ | feed | earth
sentence:
Better than a strong arm is a gentle voice,
Feeding the spirit as spring feeds the earth.

label: work | see | ∅
sentence: Fifth, the craftsman who earns his place by his hands, his work seen and respected, so that his spirit is not lost as he follows his journey.

label: king | sleep | ∅
sentence:
But when the great chief falls,
Even kings sleep hungry.

label: gift | bring | joy
sentence: These gifts bring joy on the road, but you, son, who have none, venture out with only daydreams.

label: world | turn | ∅
sentence: The world turns harshly, and the winds do not soften for those without the right paths.”

label: wise | say | ∅
sentence: The son spoke once more: “Father, the wise say fate brings food and hardship alike, yet men must seek their path and stand against the storm.

label: heart | dare | journey
sentence: Though the night may hold danger, a brave heart must dare the journey when trapped by hunger and sorrow.

label: he | suffer | pain
sentence:
When a man has fallen from honor,
Why should he suffer still more pain?

label: horizon | be | home
sentence:
The whole horizon is his home,
And the night shelters all travelers—rich and poor alike.”

With these words, the son bade his father farewell and began his journey alone.

label: he | say | ∅
sentence: He said quietly, “When luck turns its face away, a skilled man must go where no one knows his story.”

label: he | travel | ∅
sentence: He traveled until he reached the wild river, its waters roaring like thunder and tossing stones like warriors clashing.

label: many | sit | crossing
sentence: At the crossing, many sat with coins in hand, waiting for passage.

label: warrior | speak | ∅
sentence: The young warrior spoke humbly, asking to cross, but those gathered turned away.

label: strength | move | hand
sentence:
“No one crosses without tribute,
No strength can move the silent hand of gold.”

label: boatman | laugh | ∅
sentence: A boatman laughed harshly, “No money—no crossing!

label: ∅ | be | use
sentence: What use is the strength of ten men?

label: ∅ | pay | price
sentence: Pay the price.”

label: anger | flare | heart
sentence: Anger flared in the warrior’s heart.

label: he | cry | ∅
sentence: As the boat pulled away, he cried out, “If my robe can buy the way, take it and bring me across.”

label: boat | turn | ∅
sentence: The boat turned back, greed overcoming pride.

label: he | seize | collar
sentence: When the warrior reached, he seized the boatman’s collar and brought him down.

label: another | try | ∅
sentence: Another tried to help but met the same fate and fled.

label: word | lead | heart
sentence:
“Where there is strife, be gentle,
A soft word can lead the fiercest heart.

label: ∅ | lead | bear
sentence:
Lead the bear by a single hair,
Not by force that breaks the willow.”

label: boat | reach | midstream
sentence: The boat reached a lone stone pillar standing midstream, and the boatman warned, “The boat must be tied or lost.

label: warrior | climb | ∅
sentence:
Whoever is strongest, climb the pillar to fasten the cord.”

Proud and strong, the young warrior climbed.

label: boatman | let | ∅
sentence: But as he grasped the rope, the boatman let it slip and pushed the vessel away, leaving the warrior stranded.

label: hunger | chase | footstep
sentence: Days passed; hunger and thirst chased his footsteps.

label: he | sink | river
sentence: He sank into the river under the weight of exhaustion, but the Great Spirit spared him.

label: he | awake | shore
sentence: He awoke on the far shore, weak and wild-eyed, eating roots and leaves to live.

label: he | wander | desert
sentence: He wandered the desert until he found a well, where people drank and laughed.

label: he | beg | coin
sentence: He begged for a coin but was turned away.

label: he | scold | ∅
sentence: Frustrated, he scolded and fought those who mocked him, but the many were too strong, and he fell wounded.

label: bear | overcome | ∅
sentence:
Even the mightiest bear
Can be overcome by the swarm of ants.

label: they | strip | hide
sentence:
When small creatures unite,
They strip the fierce lion's hide.

label: he | join | band
sentence: Weak and alone, he joined a traveling band moving toward the night, but thieves lurked nearby, and the journey held danger.

label: other | welcome | he
sentence: The others welcomed him warmly, bringing food and water.

label: other | whisper | ∅
sentence:
Once a stranger’s friend stole his gold,
Leaving him naked and weeping at dawn.”

“Let us leave this stranger behind,” the others whispered, afraid and suspicious.

label: they | rise | sun
sentence: They rose with the sun and departed, abandoning the sleeping warrior.

label: warrior | sit | breaking
sentence: Lost and parched, the warrior sat with his heart breaking.

label: who | stand | i
sentence: “Who will stand with me once the trading horses have gone?

label: stranger | walk | shadow
sentence: A stranger walks with only a stranger’s shadow.”

label: he | speak | word
sentence: He spoke these words when a prince from a distant tribe hunting nearby found him.

label: prince | ask | story
sentence: Seeing his noble bearing beneath sorrow’s veil, the prince asked his story.

label: prince | clothe | he
sentence: Moved by his plight, the prince clothed him in fine robes, gave him precious beads, and sent a trusted guide to lead him home.

label: father | rejoice | ∅
sentence: When they returned, his father rejoiced, grateful for his safe return.

label: son | tell | boatman
sentence: That evening, the son told of the harsh boatman, the cruel desert people, and the betrayals along the path.

label: son | tell | you
sentence: “My son,” the father said, “Have I not told you that the hands of the empty are like a broken bear’s paw?”

label: son | reply | ∅
sentence: The son replied, “Father, no treasure comes without trial.

label: i | suffer | storm
sentence: I have suffered storms, but from the clouds I gathered honey.

label: say | set | table
sentence: The wise say, fate sets the table, but man must bring the fire.”

label: ∅ | be | ∅
sentence:
“To fear the crocodile,
Is to never dive for pearls.

label: stone | move | ∅
sentence:
The stone beneath the grinding mill never moves,
Yet must bear the heaviest load.

label: lion | eat | what
sentence:
What will the lion eat in his den?
What nourishment for the fallen hawk?

label: you | have | hand
sentence:
If you wish to catch game in your own lands,
You must have the hands and feet of the spider.”

label: father | nod | ∅
sentence: The father nodded, “This time, the spirits favored you, and a royal hand healed your wounds.

label: gift | be | ∅
sentence: Such gifts are rare as the morning star’s dance.”

label: story | be | time
sentence: The story is like the time a great chief placed a precious eagle feather on the village totem, promising honor to any who could shoot an arrow through its eye.

label: many | try | ∅
sentence: Many tried and missed, except a small boy, who at play let loose a random arrow, and it passed through perfectly.

label: boy | earn | feather
sentence: The boy earned the feather and gifts, but then, burning his bow and arrows, said, “Only the first light should last forever.”

label: wise | walk | trail
sentence: The wise and the foolish, the strong and the weak—all walk the long trail, seeking their place beneath the sky.
